# PulseMeter

Unofficial Windows tray HUD for Codex usage visibility. PulseMeter shows rate-limit remaining, reset credits, account usage, daily usage, estimated project usage, and sync status in a compact WPF floating window.

PulseMeter is not affiliated with OpenAI.

## Privacy Boundaries

- No telemetry.
- No maintainer-owned analytics or crash reporting.
- No Codex Desktop UI scraping.
- No OCR.
- No password, API key, or token prompts.
- Live mode may read `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\auth.json` only to request reset-credit expiry metadata.
- Live mode may read `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\state_5.sqlite` and `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\sessions` to estimate local project usage shares.
- PulseMeter should never print bearer tokens, account IDs, reset-credit IDs, local private paths, or Codex message text.

## Key Files

- `src/PulseMeter/PulseMeter.csproj`: WPF app project metadata.
- `src/PulseMeter/Bootstrap`: startup and dependency registration.
- `src/PulseMeter/Platform/Codex`: Codex CLI/app-server process and JSON-RPC integration.
- `src/PulseMeter/Slices`: feature slices for rate limits, account usage, project usage, reset credits, navigation, and the window shell.
- `tests/PulseMeter.Tests`: xUnit regression tests.
- `scripts/package-release.ps1`: portable Windows release ZIP packaging.
- `README.md`, `PRIVACY.md`, and `SECURITY.md`: user-facing behavior and trust docs.

## Commands

```powershell
dotnet test PulseMeter.slnx -c Release
dotnet build PulseMeter.slnx -c Release
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\scripts\package-release.ps1 -Version 0.5.0
```

## Contribution Rules

- Small bug fixes and documentation fixes may open a pull request directly.
- New features, architecture changes, dependency additions, and refactor-only work should start with an issue and maintainer agreement.
- Bug fixes should include a reproduction and a test when practical.
- Pull requests should include Real Behavior Proof: environment, exact command or manual steps, observed result, and what was not tested.
- Do not include tokens, auth files, account IDs, local file paths, or private Codex session contents in public issues or pull requests.

- Privacy: `PRIVACY.md`
- Security: `SECURITY.md`
